response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a strong background in web platforms, TypeScript/Node.js, and developer tooling. However, the Principal Performance Architect role requires deep hardware-level expertise — SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, SIMD/MIMD architecture, GPU workload optimization, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear anywhere in this resume. His closest relevant experience is low-latency application development at Sociomantic using async I/O and high-performance databases, but that is firmly in the software domain and does not translate to the hardware architecture competencies this role demands.
strengths: Long tenure in performance-sensitive software contexts (low-latency microservices handling millions of req/s at Sociomantic)
Broad multi-language systems experience (DLang, C/C++, TypeScript) and embedded/hardware-adjacent work at Altom and National Instruments
Active contributor to AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling, showing engagement with the AI ecosystem
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, SIMD/MIMD architectures, or GPU workload analysis — the core technical requirements of this role
No background in deep-learning training or inference optimization, nor any AI/ML hardware or accelerator work
Career trajectory is entirely software-focused (web, mobile, backend); no evidence of hardware-software co-design or silicon-level performance analysis
